verses.text: In the third month after Israel left Egypt, on that day, they came to the wilderness of Sinai. They departed from Rephidim, came to the desert of Sinai, and camped before the mount. Guidance: God's exact timing matters. Three months and a few days — Sinai is the destination Egypt itself was the journey toward. Sometimes the place God meets you is the whole reason He delivered you.

In the third month after Israel left Egypt, on that day, they came to the wilderness of Sinai. They departed from Rephidim, came to the desert of Sinai, and camped before the mount. Guidance: God's exact timing matters. Three months and a few days — Sinai is the destination Egypt itself was the journey toward. Sometimes the place God meets you is the whole reason He delivered you.

Moses went up unto God. The LORD called from the mount: thus shall you say to the house of Jacob — you have seen what I did to the Egyptians, how I bore you on eagles' wings and brought you unto Myself. Guidance: The exodus was not about leaving Egypt; it was about being carried to God. Eagles' wings is how He describes His own rescue. He didn't ask you to walk; He carried you.

Moses went up unto God. The LORD called from the mount: thus shall you say to the house of Jacob — you have seen what I did to the Egyptians, how I bore you on eagles' wings and brought you unto Myself. Guidance: The exodus was not about leaving Egypt; it was about being carried to God. Eagles' wings is how He describes His own rescue. He didn't ask you to walk; He carried you.

Now therefore, if you will obey My voice and keep My covenant, you shall be a peculiar treasure unto Me above all people — for all the earth is Mine. You shall be unto Me a kingdom of priests, a holy nation. Guidance: God calls a freed slave people His peculiar treasure. Not because they earned it, but because He chose. Identity is given before any law is spoken.

Now therefore, if you will obey My voice and keep My covenant, you shall be a peculiar treasure unto Me above all people — for all the earth is Mine. You shall be unto Me a kingdom of priests, a holy nation. Guidance: God calls a freed slave people His peculiar treasure. Not because they earned it, but because He chose. Identity is given before any law is spoken.

Moses came and called for the elders of the people and laid before them all these words. All the people answered together: all that the LORD has spoken, we will do. Moses returned the words of the people unto the LORD. Guidance: The first response to grace is a corporate yes. The people haven't even heard the law yet — they say yes to the One who carried them. Trust precedes obedience.

Moses came and called for the elders of the people and laid before them all these words. All the people answered together: all that the LORD has spoken, we will do. Moses returned the words of the people unto the LORD. Guidance: The first response to grace is a corporate yes. The people haven't even heard the law yet — they say yes to the One who carried them. Trust precedes obedience.

The LORD: lo, I come unto thee in a thick cloud, that the people may hear when I speak with thee, and believe thee for ever. And Moses told the words of the people unto the LORD. Guidance: God reveals Himself publicly so the leader's words will be trusted. Not every revelation is private; sometimes God makes Himself known in a way the whole community can witness.

The LORD: go to the people, sanctify them today and tomorrow; let them wash their clothes. Be ready by the third day, for that day the LORD will come down on Sinai. Set bounds — whoever touches the mount shall die. When the trumpet sounds long, they shall come up. Guidance: Preparation matters. The encounter with God on day three requires two days of consecration. Don't expect to feel His presence on Sunday if you haven't been ready since Friday.

Set bounds — whoever touches the mount shall die. When the trumpet sounds long, they shall come up. Guidance: Preparation matters. The encounter with God on day three requires two days of consecration. Don't expect to feel His presence on Sunday if you haven't been ready since Friday.

Moses went down from the mount unto the people, sanctified them, and they washed their clothes. He said to the people: be ready by the third day; come not at your wives. Guidance: Even good things are set aside for a season when God is approaching. Marriage is not unclean — but holiness sometimes requires temporary fasting from what is normally given.

Moses went down from the mount unto the people, sanctified them, and they washed their clothes. He said to the people: be ready by the third day; come not at your wives. Guidance: Even good things are set aside for a season when God is approaching. Marriage is not unclean — but holiness sometimes requires temporary fasting from what is normally given.

On the third day morning, thunders and lightnings, a thick cloud upon the mount, the trumpet's voice exceeding loud — all the people trembled. Moses brought them to the foot of the mount. Sinai was on smoke; the LORD descended in fire; the mount quaked. The trumpet grew louder; Moses spoke, God answered by a voice. Guidance: Holiness has weather. Hebrews later contrasts this terror with the mountain we come to in Christ. Both are true: God's nearness can shake mountains and welcome children.

The LORD came down upon Sinai; He called Moses up, and Moses went up. The LORD: go down, charge the people, lest they break through to gaze, and many perish. Let the priests who come near also sanctify themselves, lest the LORD break forth upon them. Guidance: Spiritual hunger can become dangerous when it forgets reverence. Even priests must consecrate themselves — proximity to God is not a casual privilege.

Let the priests who come near also sanctify themselves, lest the LORD break forth upon them. Guidance: Spiritual hunger can become dangerous when it forgets reverence. Let the priests who come near also sanctify themselves, lest the LORD break forth upon them. Guidance: Spiritual hunger can become dangerous when it forgets reverence. Even priests must consecrate themselves — proximity to God is not a casual privilege.

Moses: the people cannot come up to Sinai; You charged us to set bounds. The LORD: get down. You and Aaron may come up, but let not the priests and people break through, lest He break forth upon them. So Moses went down and spoke to them. Guidance: Even Moses argues a moment with God about protocol, and God patiently sends him back down. Be willing to deliver instructions a second time when the people have to hear them again.
